svg.text: Runs on separate CUDA stream — resolves keys to flat-table slot positions
Hit → slot in HBM cache. Miss → storage fallback needed.
LRU/LFU eviction: make room for new keys. Evicted data must be persisted.
Write-back: persist evicted cache entries to storage before overwrite.
[HBM Direct Path — no cache, storage on HBM]
No cache. Direct hash table lookup on HBM storage. Simpler path.
New admitted keys get default-initialized embedding + optimizer state values.
Cache Path vs HBM Direct Path?
Cache: best for skewed (Zipf) access. Hot keys in HBM, cold in storage. Write-back eviction.
HBM Direct: uniform access or table fits in HBM. No eviction overhead. Simpler control flow.
Prefetch overlaps with previous batch's compute, hiding hash lookup + cache miss + I/O latency.
